svg.title: Payment System HLD with Fallback Mechanisms
svg.desc: Complete payment system high-level design with fallback and crash recovery for gateway routing, Redis, PostgreSQL, Kafka, fraud detection, refund service, and ledger failures.

g.text: JWT auth, TLS termination, rate limiting
Step 1: Check idempotency key — already processed?
Step 2: Run validation chain (amount / method / fraud)
Step 3: Persist txn as INITIATED in DB
Step 4: Route to correct payment gateway
Step 5: Update status SUCCESS / FAILED → notify
Any failure → short-circuit, return error immediately
UPI → Razorpay | Card → Stripe / Razorpay | Wallet → Internal
SUCCESS → txn marked SUCCESS + gatewayTxnId stored
FAILED → txn marked FAILED + failureReason stored

svg.text: — fallback zone 1: API gateway / payment service crash —

g.text: Crash scenario A — gateway or payment service down
Crash scenario B — Razorpay / Stripe / wallet down
Validate: txn in SUCCESS / PARTIAL_REFUND state
Route to same gateway that processed original txn
Notify payer: refund initiated — 3–5 business days

svg.text: — fallback zone 3: refund service / gateway failure —

g.text: Crash scenario C — refund service or gateway down
Each leg is an independent transaction
Leg 1 SUCCESS, Leg 2 FAILED → rollback Leg 1
All legs must succeed or all are refunded
Each leg has its own idempotency key (retryable)

svg.text: — fallback zone 4: saga coordinator or leg crash —

g.text: Crash scenario D — saga coordinator dies mid-flow

svg.text: — fallback zone 5: Redis / webhook / idempotency failure —

g.text: Crash scenario E — Redis down or webhook delivery fails

svg.text: — fallback zone 6: PostgreSQL / primary DB crash —

g.text: Crash scenario F — primary PostgreSQL down

svg.text: — fallback zone 7: Kafka / ledger / Cassandra failure —

g.text: Crash scenario G — event bus or ledger down
The 4 rules every payment system must enforce
1. Idempotency: same key = same result, zero double charges even on retry
2. Record first: persist txn as INITIATED before calling gateway — never lose a transaction
3. Money as integers: store in paise / cents — never use floating point for money
4. Webhook verification: always verify HMAC signature — never trust unverified callbacks
